demographics.living_situation: lives at home; an adult child is present in the house

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Call made by the patient's adult child] Please help — he clutched his chest, fell to the floor, and now he won't wake up and he's making horrible gasping noises! He's not answering me and his lips are going blue!
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My dad suddenly collapsed a few minutes ago and won't respond — I don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: very sudden — he grabbed his chest and dropped to the floor about 4 minutes ago
symptom_narrative.duration: unresponsive for the last few minutes and not getting better
symptom_narrative.character: he collapsed all at once; now he's floppy, won't wake, and only makes occasional gasping sounds
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst thing I've ever seen — he looks lifeless
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: he's on the living room floor
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since he collapsed, no change
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — shaking him and shouting his name does nothing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he said his chest hurt badly right before he went down; now his lips and face look blue-grey
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't think this has ever happened before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is completely unresponsive and not moving

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home when he collapsed; his adult child is with him and made the call, and is in severe shock and panicking

objective_data_offered.value: he doesn't answer when I shout his name or shake his shoulders — no response at all
he's not breathing normally — just occasional irregular gasps every few seconds
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lay carer describing agonal gasps under extreme distress — easily mistaken for 'still breathing'

objective_data_offered.value: his lips and face are going blue-grey
he's lying flat and completely floppy on the living room floor
there's clear space around him on the floor and the front door can be opened for help
